summary: Matched and linked MSL/math.c by converting it from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py and making targeted codegen/data-layout changes in logf and related constants. The final fix centered on placing logf constants and float special values in math.c/.sdata2, adjusting union field order, inlining polynomial temporaries, adding explicit float casts for E, and removing obsolete coefficient globals from math_data.c. The PR body notes that the remaining mismatch required "a bit of inlining" after automated attempts failed.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object("MSL/math.c") is built as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Reworked src/MSL/math.c constants: replaced runtime float_nan and float_inf expressions with SECTION_SDATA2 globals, including MSL_Math_804DE1B0, MSL_Math_804DE1B4, __float_nan, and __float_huge.
Added a SECTION_SDATA2 macro gated on __MWERKS__ and not M2CTX to force selected globals into .sdata2 for the target compiler while keeping non-Metrowerks tooling compatible.
Changed FloatU32 union member order from float/u32 to u32/float, allowing initializers such as { 0x7FFFFFFF } and { 0x7F800000 } to define special float bit patterns.
Moved logf Taylor coefficient bit-pattern storage out of math_data.c and into math.c as named MSL_Math_804DE1B0 and MSL_Math_804DE1B4 globals.

Simplified and reshaped logf codegen: switched directly on (*(u32*) &x & EXP_MASK), introduced raw_fm/raw_fM declarations in the outer normal-case block, used register float temporaries for H and h2_poly, inlined the polynomial expression, and explicitly cast E to float in LN2 multiplication.
Changed exceptional logf returns to use __float_nan.f and __float_huge.f instead of float_nan and float_inf.
Removed an old explanatory nonmatching assembly note about array base-pointer generation for __one_over_F and __ln_F, implying that the previous mismatch was resolved.
smart_moves: Used compiler/translation-unit data placement control instead of algorithmic changes: SECTION_SDATA2 preserved target layout for constants while avoiding disruption to M2CTX and non-Metrowerks builds.
Kept lookup tables in math_data.c but moved scalar coefficients into math.c, balancing the existing need to prevent compiler table coalescing with the need for matching scalar global loads.
Represented NaN and infinity through exact u32 bit patterns in a union rather than relying on platform-dependent expressions like 0.0 / 0.0 or overflowing constants.
Adjusted expression shape and temporary lifetimes in logf rather than changing the math, using inlining and register hints to steer instruction selection and scheduling.
Removed obsolete debug/nonmatching notes after the object became matching, keeping the source cleaner and more searchable for future work.
decomp_lessons: For MSL floating-point code, exact constant placement and representation can be as important as numeric equivalence; use bit-pattern globals when target code loads constants as raw words.

When matching Metrowerks output, small C source changes such as union member order, temporary scope, explicit casts, and inlining can affect register allocation and instruction ordering enough to close final diffs.
Guard Metrowerks-specific attributes with M2CTX-aware preprocessor checks so headers/source remain usable by analysis tooling.

Separating large lookup tables into a different compilation unit can prevent unwanted compiler coalescing, but scalar constants may still need to live in the function's compilation unit to match target loads.
If an automated decomp attempt stalls on a final mismatch, inspect whether expression inlining or temporary elimination better matches the original compiler's emitted code.
naming_conventions: Generated or address-derived MSL globals used the form MSL_Math_804DE1B0 and MSL_Math_804DE1B4.
Internal MSL special float constants used double-underscore names: __float_nan and __float_huge.
Existing lookup table naming remained double-underscore plus descriptive suffix, such as __ln_F, __one_over_F, __sincos_on_quadrant, and __sincos_poly.
Bit-cast helper and exponent/mantissa macros remained all-caps, e.g. F32_BIT_CAST, F32_UNBIASED_EXPONENT, F32_HIGH_MANTISSA_BITS, and F32_LOW_MANTISSA_BITS.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed MSL/math.c from NonMatching to Matching only after source-level codegen changes were sufficient.
Used __declspec(section ".sdata2") under __MWERKS__ to force constants into the small-data float constant section.
Used u32 bit-pattern constants plus FloatU32 reinterpretation to produce exact special float values without relying on host/platform floating behavior.
Inlined the logf polynomial calculation as H * H * (H * *(float*) &coef[1] + *(float*) &coef[0]) instead of keeping separate C1, C0, new_var, and poly temporaries.
Added register float declarations for H and h2_poly to influence allocation/codegen in the hot logf normal path.
Applied explicit (float) E casts in LN2 multiplication to match expected floating-point conversion/codegen.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states that Codex ran for over an hour but did not solve the final mismatch; the author reports the final fix was a small amount of inlining.
follow_up_queries: Find other MSL objects still using platform-dependent NaN or infinity expressions instead of exact bit-pattern constants.
Search for remaining NonMatching MSL source files in configure.py that may need similar .sdata2 or inlining tactics.
Audit M2CTX guards around __declspec(section ".sdata2") to ensure analysis tooling compatibility is preserved.
Compare uses of scalar coefficient globals in math/trig sources to see whether they should live with the consuming function or in a separate data compilation unit.
